title: Saturday morning, no plans, full tank
excerpt: No destination. No group. Just the bike, a jacket that smells like two-stroke and regret, and the whole weekend stretching out ahead.
content:
No destination. No group. Just the bike, a jacket that smells like two-stroke and regret, and the whole weekend stretching out ahead.

I've been riding long enough to know that the rides with no plan are the best ones. You end up in places you'd never Google. A roadside servo that still has a Paddle Pop sign from 1994. A lookout with a bench someone dragged up themselves. A town with one pub and a water tower painted like a cricket ball.

I stopped for a pie somewhere past the back blocks. The guy behind the counter asked where I was headed. I said I didn't really know. He nodded like that was the most reasonable thing he'd heard all week.

I think I'm going to keep doing this. Every Saturday I can. No route. No group chat. Just go.

The bike doesn't care where we end up. Neither do I.
